summary: Implementation Design audited the Coding/Repair rework for the sec-e2e-027 regression and sec-e2e-028 stability. The frozen tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py plus tests/integration/security/harness.py contracts remain intact, focused sec-e2e-027 and sec-e2e-028 both pass, tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py passes 5/5, npm run test:argo passes 5/5, and design/KG/test-failure-records.json is empty. No production rework remains for Coding/Repair; this handoff is ready for final intention-design audit.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real-app subprocess acceptance entrypoint: authenticate an employee, delegate a high-risk action through the agent/plugin/tool path, and provide the required confirmation through the protected harness.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: Security Center and the audit query surface must expose a continuous user -> agent -> plugin -> tool evidence chain with a durable confirmation artifact digest and no anonymous or fallback-system identity link.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real-app subprocess acceptance entrypoint: execute three high-risk actions to create a multi-record committed audit baseline, edit the second committed non-tail audit record by operating-system means while later records remain present, then attempt another high-risk action without manual trust recovery.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: The system must detect historical-record ledger divergence before releasing the next high-risk boundary, raise one business-level data-continuity anomaly, enter local UNTRUSTED lock mode, refuse sensitive tooling, and keep the frozen Security Center backend plus operator-web observation surfaces recovery-required or UNTRUSTED rather than CLEAR until full-chain cloud validation succeeds.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real-app subprocess acceptance entrypoint: let a trusted client miss the lease window, observe denied rejoin before missing-gap verification, then drive the distinct recovery control point and restored-access attempt.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: Security Center must mark the same canonical client UNTRUSTED after lease expiry, the pre-recovery console status must show denied rejoin before proof, the recovery control point must accept full-chain missing-gap validation, and the post-recovery trust projection plus console access must become ALIGNED or TRUSTED/200 only after continuity validation.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real-app subprocess acceptance entrypoint: establish trusted audit-head continuity, stop the runtime through a normal offline path without mutating local audit evidence, restart the same canonical client before lease expiry, and attempt ordinary model access.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: Security Center backend and operator web must show the same canonical client as ALIGNED or TRUSTED with gap_status=CLEAR, recovery_gate_status=CLEAR, recovery_required=false, no missing-gap validation incident, and ordinary model access must return 200.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real-app subprocess acceptance entrypoint: submit nested deceptive instructions claiming test or maintenance mode while targeting a High risk tool without trusted provenance or confirmation.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: The high-risk tool must remain blocked at the tool-call boundary, rejection must be recorded as durable security evidence with trace-bound nonce semantics, and Security Center/operator surfaces must expose the rejected attempt rather than silently executing.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-audit-contract-boundaries
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Read OVERALL_ARCHITECTURE.md, src/qwenpaw/security/ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy/ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy/api/ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy/web/ARCHITECTURE.md, tests/ARCHITECTURE.md, and tests/integration/security/ARCHITECTURE.md.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard proves the explicit security entrypoints remain owned by the runtime security boundary, separate Security Center deployment boundary, and tests architecture contract.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-explicit-entrypoint-traceability
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Read SystemArchitecture.json, ImplementationToCodingHandoff.json, test-failure-records.json, tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py, and tests/integration/security/harness.py.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard proves sec-e2e-024/sec-e2e-025/sec-e2e-027/sec-e2e-028/sec-e2e-021 acceptanceCriteria, handoff entryPath, failure record path, GIVEN/WHEN/THEN markers, harness method names, and business failure category strings remain frozen.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-runtime-client-identity-boundary
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Read src/qwenpaw/security/audit_foundation.py and deploy/api/store.py to compare the client id used by startup heartbeat against the client ids used by recovery, lockdown, and restored-access projection.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard proves one live runtime must use one canonical Security Center client id across heartbeat, recovery, lockdown, restored-access projection, and now normal-offline reconnect CLEAR projection.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-runtime-lease-persistence-boundary
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Read deploy/api/app.py and deploy/api/store.py to confirm the recovery-handshake HTTP contract carries lease_ttl_seconds and the durable Security Center store writes last_heartbeat_at, lease_ttl_seconds, and lease_expires_at.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard proves durable lease persistence stays in the API/store seam and cannot be replaced by projection-only fallback while repairing sec-e2e-028.

supportingNonExplicitTests.name: security-config-regression
supportingNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Exercise repository-owned security configuration surfaces while Coding/Repair fixes the full explicit gate.
supportingNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: Security configuration regressions are caught without widening or weakening explicit acceptance entrypoints.

supportingNonExplicitTests.name: lease-recovery-semantics-contract
supportingNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Exercise Security Center lease timing, startup heartbeat, canonical runtime identity, TTL downgrade, and reconnect recovery semantics beneath sec-e2e-027 and sec-e2e-028.
supportingNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The contract tests keep lease-expiry recovery separate from normal-offline reconnect CLEAR semantics while Coding/Repair restores the explicit sec-e2e-028 entrypoint.

codingTargets.failureSignal: resolved: sec-e2e-027 now passes unchanged, design/KG/test-failure-records.json is empty, and no open Coding/Repair failure signal remains.
codingTargets.nextAction: No Coding/Repair production rework remains. Keep the frozen explicit entrypoints and protected harness unchanged, preserve sec-e2e-027 block-first/release-after-proof semantics plus sec-e2e-028 normal reconnect CLEAR stability, and pass this resolved state to @intention-design for final intent audit.

taskExecutionPlan.executionStrategy: Treat sec-e2e-027 and sec-e2e-028 as resolved by the audited Coding/Repair rework. Preserve sec-e2e-025 tamper lockdown, sec-e2e-027 lease-expiry block-first/release-after-proof, and sec-e2e-028 normal reconnect CLEAR semantics. No further production changes are authorized by this handoff unless final intention-design audit reopens the intent boundary.

tasks.title: Keep lease and reconnect stable
tasks.objective: Preserve sec-e2e-027 block-first/release-after-proof behavior and sec-e2e-028 normal reconnect CLEAR behavior after the startup/readiness and shutdown-budget repair.
steps: Keep tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py, tests/integration/security/harness.py, and tests/integration/conftest.py read-only; do not xfail, skip, or weaken Lease_Expiry_Active_Defense_Gap or Normal_Offline_Reconnect_Clear_State_Gap assertions.

Preserve the lightweight security startup path: unauthenticated integration-mode Security Center runtime may serve HTTP readiness, heartbeat, and recovery paths before heavy agent/plugin preload completes.
For true lease expiry, pre-proof reconnect must deny model access at the security boundary before missing-gap proof.
After valid missing-gap verification for the same canonical client, deploy/api and runtime projection must move to ALIGNED or TRUSTED with recovery_required=false and restored model access 200.

For normal reconnect before lease expiry, Security Center backend/web must remain ALIGNED or TRUSTED with gap_status=CLEAR, recovery_gate_status=CLEAR, recovery_required=false, and ordinary model access 200.
Rerun focused sec-e2e-027, focused sec-e2e-028, tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py -q, and npm run test:argo before any future release affecting this boundary.
tasks.completionSignal: Focused sec-e2e-027 and sec-e2e-028 both pass unchanged, and npm run test:argo exits 0 with design/KG/test-failure-records.json empty.

tasks.title: Preserve full explicit gate
tasks.objective: Keep all repository-declared explicit testcases passing together after the sec-e2e-027 and sec-e2e-028 stability repair.
steps: Run the focused sec-e2e-027 pytest entrypoint unchanged.
Run the focused sec-e2e-028 pytest entrypoint unchanged and keep it below the Argo 120s budget.
Run npm run validate:system-architecture and npm run validate:handoff:implementation.
Run npm run test:argo so design/KG/test-failure-records.json is refreshed by the architecture runner.
If a future failure appears, update design/KG/test-failure-records.json with the failing entrypoint, command, exit code, business category, and first actionable failure message.
Do not remove sec-e2e-028 from SystemArchitecture.json or from the explicit pytest command to make the gate pass.
tasks.completionSignal: The focused sec-e2e-027 and sec-e2e-028 entrypoints, handoff validation, and Argo explicit gate exit 0 with no stale failure record.

openGaps: No open sec-e2e-027 or sec-e2e-028 implementation architecture gap remains after the audited Coding/Repair rework. Focused sec-e2e-027 passed in 39.40s, focused sec-e2e-028 passed in 58.59s, tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py passed 5/5 in 74.06s, and npm run test:argo passed 5/5 with design/KG/test-failure-records.json empty.

SystemArchitecture.json contains sec-e2e-027 and sec-e2e-028 acceptanceCriteria as frozen tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py entrypoints; Implementation Design did not modify the intent graph during this audit.
Final @intention-design audit should verify that the repaired production behavior still matches the intent distinction between lease-expiry active defense and normal offline reconnect.
